projectDescription: Stlite runs Streamlit apps entirely in the browser using Pyodide (WebAssembly), eliminating the need for a Python server.
keyFeatures: Run Streamlit apps entirely in the browser with no Python server required
Powered by Pyodide (CPython compiled to WebAssembly)
Embed via CDN with a single <script> tag or the mount() JS function
Bundle Streamlit apps as standalone Electron desktop apps via @stlite/desktop
CLI tool to export apps as shareable URLs, self-contained HTML, or offline web bundles
Install Python packages at runtime via micropip
SharedWorker mode for running multiple apps with reduced memory
publicApiSurface: mount(options, container) — @stlite/browser: mounts a Streamlit app on a DOM element
<streamlit-app> custom element — HTML tag-based API for embedding apps
<app-file>, <app-requirements> — child tags for multi-file app configuration
createKernel(options) — @stlite/react: creates a Pyodide kernel instance
StliteApp — @stlite/react: React component rendering a Streamlit app
StliteAppWithToast — @stlite/react: StliteApp with progress/error toast notifications
useKernel() — @stlite/react: React hook for kernel lifecycle management
stliteReactPlugin() — Vite plugin for @stlite/react bundling
controller.unmount() — disposes the kernel and unmounts the React root
controller.install(requirements, options?) — installs Python packages at runtime
controller.writeFile(path, data, opts?) — writes a file to the virtual FS
controller.readFile(path, opts?) — reads a file from the virtual FS
controller.renameFile(oldPath, newPath) — renames a file in the virtual FS
controller.unlink(path) — deletes a file from the virtual FS
controller.runPython(code) — executes Python code in the Pyodide environment
controller.getCodeCompletion(code, position) — language-server code completion
stlite share <path> — CLI: encode project as a share.stlite.net URL
stlite html <path> — CLI: export project as a self-contained HTML file
stlite web <path> — CLI: package project as an offline-capable web directory
stlite desktop <path> — CLI: package project as an Electron desktop artifact
